---
title: "Contributing"
description: "How to contribute to MyProject."
---

We welcome contributions of all sizes — bug reports, documentation improvements, or new features.

## Getting started

1. **Fork** the repository on GitHub and clone your fork locally.
2. Install dependencies with `npm install`.
3. Create a feature branch: `git checkout -b feat/my-feature`.

## Development workflow

```bash
npm run dev      # start the dev server with hot reload
npm test         # run the full test suite
npm run lint     # check code style
npm run build    # build for production
```

::: {.callout-important}
Please ensure all tests pass (`npm test`) before opening a pull request.
:::

## Pull request guidelines

- Keep PRs focused — one feature or fix per PR.
- Write or update tests for any changed behaviour.
- Update the relevant documentation in the `docs/` folder.
- Use a clear, descriptive PR title (e.g. `feat: add lazy() timeout option`).

## Commit style

We follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/):

| Prefix | Use for |
|--------|---------|
| `feat:` | New features |
| `fix:` | Bug fixes |
| `docs:` | Documentation only |
| `chore:` | Tooling, CI, dependencies |
| `refactor:` | Code changes with no behaviour change |

## Code of conduct

This project follows the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/). Be kind and constructive.
